pattern: Sort the array, then pair the smallest with the largest iteratively — the minimum of all pairwise averages is always the first pair (smallest + largest) / 2.
hints: After sorting, the smallest element is at index 0 and the largest at index n-1. Pairing them gives the smallest possible average, since any other pairing mixes a larger small element with a smaller large one.
Compute (nums[i] + nums[n-1-i]) / 2 for i = 0 .. n/2 - 1 and return the minimum.
The minimum will always be the first pair (i=0) because nums is sorted and nums[0] + nums[n-1] <= nums[i] + nums[n-1-i] for all i. Still compute all to be safe.

edge_inputs.description: all same value: average is that value
odd-looking but min is clearly first pair

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size // 2) * 2  # even length
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
